title: Tournesol verbascifolia
taxonomy: Tournesol verbascifolia, also known as Yellow Hollyhock or False Mullein, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. It is a member of the genus Verbascifolia, which is part of the order Lamiales. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of the Balkans and is known for its tall, stately growth and bright yellow flowers. The classification of Tournesol verbascifolia is further supported by its leaf structure and other morphological characteristics. This plant is also often confused with the similar-looking Mullein species due to its similarities in appearance.
